description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden enclosure and a classic mid-century design. The front panel features a large horizontal frequency display with white numerals and markings, flanked by a series of push buttons and three prominent cylindrical tuning knobs. The speaker grille is integrated into the left side of the front panel, consisting of a series of vertical slits. The sides and top are finished in a warm, wood veneer, while the back panel has ventilation slots and several ports for power and antenna connections. The overall appearance suggests a sturdy, well-crafted appliance typical of mid-20th-century home electronics.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a horizontal frequency display, three tuning knobs, and a front speaker grille. The design is classic and robust, with a warm wood finish and functional control elements.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency display, three tuning knobs, push buttons, and the speaker grille on the left.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the smooth, uninterrupted wood finish and the protruding tuning knobs.
description_view_2: The rear view displays ventilation slots, power and antenna ports, and a plain wood surface.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood finish and the ends of the tuning knobs.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
